reasoning: The baseline rating on the Wikipedia page on 2025-05-11 is 2751. A 1% increase requires Wesley So's rating to reach at least 2778.51, which effectively means achieving a rating of 2779. FIDE ratings are only updated on the 1st of each month. Therefore, on 2025-05-18, the official rating listed on the Wikipedia page will remain exactly 2751, making a 1% increase virtually impossible (0% chance under normal circumstances, factoring in slight risks of page vandalism or unexpected mid-month updates gives 2%).   For subsequent dates (June, August, November 2025), So would need to actively gain 28 rating points. In the modern chess elite at the 2750+ level, gaining nearly 30 points is notoriously difficult and requires dominant performances (typically +5 or higher) in super-tournaments. Over recent years, Wesley So's classical playstyle has been noted for its extraordinary solidity and high draw rate, rarely resulting in drastic rating swings in either direction. While a steady climb is possible over the long term, doing so within 1-6 months is highly improbable. As the forecast horizon extends to November, the variance increases, leading to a slightly higher estimate.
